title: GitHub Projects
content:
### GitHub Projects (Beta)

Projects provide a flexible Kanban-style view for planning and tracking work across repositories. Access from the **Projects** tab.

### Project Views

- **Table view** — Spreadsheet-style with custom fields, sorting, and filtering
- **Board view** — Kanban columns (To do, In progress, Done)
- **Timeline view** — Gantt-chart style for scheduling
- **Roadmap view** — High-level timeline of items

### Custom Fields

Add fields like text, number, date, single select, and iteration to track metadata directly on project items.

### Automation

Use built-in workflows: automatically move issues to "In Progress" when assigned, or to "Done" when closed. You can also trigger GitHub Actions from project updates.

### Linking Issues and PRs

Drag and drop issues/PRs into project views. Items stay synced — closing an issue updates the project card automatically.

quiz.question: Which project view provides a Kanban column layout?
What are custom metadata fields in GitHub Projects called?
